page.title: Inversion After Negative Adverbials
page.subtitle_html: Inversion After Negative Adverbials - C1 fronted negative and limiting adverbials with auxiliary inversion.
page.subtitle_text: C1 fronted negative and limiting adverbials with auxiliary inversion.

blocks.body: {"level":"C1","intro":"Inversion After Negative Adverbials: compact C1 anchors for never, rarely, hardly, no sooner, not only, and only then.","rules":[{"label":"Negative adverbials","text":"After never, rarely, and seldom at the front, use auxiliary inversion.","example":"Never have I seen such a result."},{"label":"Hardly and no sooner","text":"Use past perfect inversion before when or than.","example":"No sooner had they left than the phone rang."},{"label":"Limiting expressions","text":"Use inversion after not only, under no circumstances, at no time, only then, and only after.","example":"Only then did I realize the mistake."}]}

blocks.heading: C1 focus
blocks.body: This compact page exists as the theory anchor for the Polyglot English C1 lesson package.
